heading: Powers
label: 140103 Powers
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)14010336:57b(b)–(j).–(j), .Aug. 4, 1937, ch. 564, § 3(b)50 Stat. 559In clause (1), the word “amend” is substituted for “alter” for consistency in the revised title. The words “not inconsistent with the Constitution and laws of the United States or of any State” are omitted as unnecessary.Clause (6) is substituted for “acquire, by purchase, devise, bequest, gift, or otherwise, and hold, encumber, convey, or otherwise dispose of such real and personal property as may be necessary or appropriate for its corporate purposes” for consistency in the revised title and to eliminate unnecessary words.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)14010336:57b(b)–(j).–(j), .Aug. 4, 1937, ch. 564, § 3(b)50 Stat. 559In clause (1), the word “amend” is substituted for “alter” for consistency in the revised title. The words “not inconsistent with the Constitution and laws of the United States or of any State” are omitted as unnecessary.Clause (6) is substituted for “acquire, by purchase, devise, bequest, gift, or otherwise, and hold, encumber, convey, or otherwise dispose of such real and personal property as may be necessary or appropriate for its corporate purposes” for consistency in the revised title and to eliminate unnecessary words.

children.text: adopt and alter a corporate seal;
appoint or elect officers and agents;
choose a board of trustees, consisting of at least 5 but not more than 15 individuals, to conduct the business and exercise the powers of the corporation;
establish and maintain offices to conduct its activities;
acquire, own, lease, encumber, and transfer property as necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of the corporation;
charge and collect membership dues and receive contributions of money or property to be devoted to carrying out the purposes of the corporation;
do any other act necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of the corporation.
